notes: Same LDS framework and layer taxonomy; Xcind adds a two-track (Diataxis user + LDS engineering) split, drops Scind's migration/confidence classification, Tier-2 tooling, and detailed Layer-6 guidance.
Both are layer-index landing pages; Xcind reframes as the 'engineering track' implementing the Scind spec and adds Behaviors + Maintenance rows and a user-docs pointer.
Trivial one-item index pointing to overview.md; substance identical.

Shared design concepts (workspace, pure overlay, two-layer networking, Traefik) match; component architecture diverges Go-vs-Bash: Scind Config Reader/State Manager/Generate Engine + state.yaml/workspaces.yaml vs Xcind three scripts/lib layout + GENERATE/EXECUTE hook pipeline + SHA cache.
Same product-index contents; Xcind adds an 'implements the Scind spec' note.
Same tool-comparison intent; Xcind adds Xcind and Scind as distinct columns plus a whole 'Xcind vs Scind' section positioning itself as the lightweight Bash/.xcind.sh implementation of Go/YAML Scind.

Shared core terms match; term drift: Scind's Exported Service/Service Contract/Flavor/Manifest/Visibility/Port Type/Protocol absent, replaced by Xcind's Export/Hook/App Root/Variable Expansion/Primary Export and a redefined Override File.
Both forward-looking 'Future' lists (not built-state); Xcind's is a strict subset keeping Application Dependencies + Health Checks and dropping Scind's Port Type Plugins and Shared Volumes.

Same problem/gap scenario; framing diverges: Scind is a formal PRD (workspace-orchestration system, three YAML schemas/flavors/service contracts) vs Xcind 'slim shell wrapper around docker compose' narrowing scope.
Identical template body; Scind keeps a trailing HTML comment Xcind drops.
Structural index; differs because ADR sets differ. Xcind README adds Scind-origin note and lists 0001-0020.
Same decision; Xcind adds worktree-instance update note (see 0019 canon-change against this ADR).
Identical but for proxy-network name prefix (scind-proxy vs xcind-proxy).
Identical decision.
Same decision; Xcind adds additive configurable URL templates (XCIND_*_URL_TEMPLATE).
Same decision; state mechanism differs (YAML state.yaml/workspaces.yaml vs .xcind.sh + .xcind/generated cache).
Same three-tier decision; Bash scripts vs YAML mechanism.
Same proxied/assigned decision; Xcind's positional-primary rule here is the root of the apex divergence (0013/0017).
Identical decision.
Same auto/custom/disabled modes; Xcind adds per-export tls key + wildcard detail (additive). Corrected later by Xcind 0016.
Identical decision.
No Xcind ADR for --workspace/--app targeting; Xcind CLI uses subcommands + DIR. Concept not captured in an Xcind ADR.
Same LDS decision, off-by-one number; minor authority-hierarchy wording diff.

Conflicting apex-designation mechanism: Scind mandates explicit primary:true and EXPLICITLY REJECTS Xcind's positional rule (0013:9,31); Xcind uses positional first-proxied (0007), and 0017 adds only apex reporting. Seed corrected: DIVERGED not RENUMBERED.
Same decision, different number; Xcind adds impl detail (compose.host-gateway.yaml, yq soft-dep, XCIND_HOST_GATEWAY*).
CLI --generate-* flag unification (replaces --generate-ide-configuration); no Scind analog.
Diataxis user track + LDS engineering track; extends Xcind 0011/Scind 0012 LDS. No Scind analog.
ports/urls/exports subcommands + xcind-config --json contract. No Scind analog.
Corrects a wildcard TLS-matching flaw in shared 0009 (*.localhost rejected by strict TLS); new default localhost.scind.io. A learning that refines shared 0009 canon - candidate P4/P3.
Ports a Scind spec-level design (no Scind ADR) with divergences (positional apex, _HOST_PORT extension). _HOST_PORT is a PROMOTE candidate.

XCIND-ONLY file, but its learning is a CANON-CHANGE against Scind 0001 (project-name isolation assumes one working copy). ADR self-classifies as canon-change, not divergence. Feeds P6/P3, not merely P4.
Opt-in host-view env file (XCIND_HOST_ENV_FILE) extending 0018; ADR self-flags as a PROMOTE candidate for Scind canon.
Both are the spec index/TOC; contents differ only by each tree's inventory (Scind lists 4 extra specs; Xcind lists hook-lifecycle + application-lifecycle).

Same topic, divergent substance: Scind three YAML schemas + flavors + primary:true vs Xcind three .xcind.sh levels + GENERATE/EXECUTE hook-pipeline table. Xcind ahead on pipeline (P4); flavors Scind-only (P5).

Same topic, different mechanism: Scind walks for workspace.yaml/application.yaml; Xcind walks for single .xcind.sh, checks parent XCIND_IS_WORKSPACE, adds self-declaration/late-bind + XCIND_APP_ROOT override. Xcind ahead.

Divergent layout: Scind .generated/*.override.yaml + overrides/ under ~/.config/scind; Xcind flat .xcind.sh + SHA-keyed .xcind/generated/{sha}/ with 8 overlays + cache, XDG state split, workspaceless mode. Xcind ahead.

Conflicting label schema beyond scind.->xcind. rename: Scind .proxy.{proto}.visibility/.url + .port.{n}.assigned + visibility labels; Xcind drops visibility, uses .http.url/.https.url/preferred .url, adds traefik.docker.network + .service + redirect middleware + two-router model. Xcind ahead on routers (P4); visibility Scind-only (P3 learning).

SCIND_->XCIND_ rename plus Xcind far ahead: _HOST_PORT for assigned exports (self-flagged as a Scind divergence), own-app scope, XCIND_HOST_ENV_FILE/_MODE, XCIND_INSTANCE/_AUTO, XCIND_NO_REGISTRY, workspace aliases, app-env injection. Scind TRAEFIK_IMAGE/SCIND_CONFIG_DIR have no Xcind analog.

Divergent generation model: Scind one monolithic {app}.override.yaml + manual overrides/; Xcind 8 per-hook SHA-keyed overlays with caching/.complete-marker/XCIND_HOOKS_ALWAYS contract + defined merge order. Xcind substantially ahead.

Same core patterns; Xcind adds XCIND_INSTANCE fold into project/workspace-network names, workspaceless-mode templates, configurable URL templates. Scind primary:true field + git-submodule strategy have no Xcind analog. Xcind ahead (instance token).

Same proxied/assigned split, divergent expression: Scind YAML exported_services + visibility + tcp/postgresql SNI (future); Xcind XCIND_PROXY_EXPORTS string syntax + per-export tls + port inference + assigned lifecycle. Xcind ahead on tls/lifecycle (P4); tcp-SNI + visibility Scind-only (P5).

Xcind substantially ahead: configurable HTTP/HTTPS ports, multi-label domain constraint (ADR-0016), layered TLS-mode resolution, config/state dir split, assigned-ports lifecycle, auto-start EXECUTE hook. Scind keeps always-on dashboard/443 + .scind.test + dnsmasq.

Divergent lifecycle model: Scind full state machine (dormant/starting/running/error) + staleness detection + generate/destroy + flavors; Xcind deliberately stateless (init/status/list/register/forget, workspaces.tsv registry, 'just rm the dir'). Xcind ahead on registry (P4); state-machine/destroy/flavor Scind-only (P5).

No Xcind state-management spec. Assigned-port stickiness partly maps to Xcind port-types/workspace-lifecycle, but flavors, port_inventory, and the assigned/unavailable/released status-transition model are Scind-only concepts absent from Xcind by design.

No dedicated Xcind spec, but the topic IS built (compose.host-gateway.yaml via xcind-host-gateway-hook); Xcind generated-override-files.md defers to this Scind spec for the detection algorithm. Built, not re-specified. Candidate gap: Scind mandates exposing *_HOST_GATEWAY env var into containers; Xcind docs don't mention injecting it.

Xcind deliberately has NO manifest - application-lifecycle.md explicitly states 'no manifest.' Deliberate architectural divergence: Xcind derives topology from Docker/filesystem on demand. Candidate P7 divergence.

No Xcind shell-integration spec. Architecturally diverged: Scind = scind-compose shell function + 'scind compose-prefix' + completion delegation (Cobra binary); Xcind = xcind-compose is itself a real binary. Xcind ships completions but never specced this design. Candidate P7 divergence.

Confirmed Xcind-only. Scind generation is monolithic (workspace generate); Xcind formalizes a CONFIGURED/RESOLVED/GENERATE/EXECUTE hook pipeline with caching, yq soft-skip/hard-fail policy, custom-hook registration. Built, unspecified in canon.

Confirmed Xcind-only. Scind folds app lifecycle into workspace-lifecycle/configuration-schemas; Xcind gives it a dedicated spec + xcind-application init/status/list/ports/urls/exports and standalone/workspace/self-declared arrangements.

Mirrors monolithic-vs-hook divergence: Scind example is the whole merged override (networks + env + visibility labels + HTTPS-only router); Xcind example is only the compose.proxy.yaml hook slice (http+https routers, xcind.export.*.url labels). Xcind ahead.

Same Traefik compose shape; Xcind conditionalizes 443/certs/dynamic mounts on TLS mode and the dashboard on XCIND_PROXY_DASHBOARD, parameterizes via XCIND_PROXY_* vars. Scind hard-codes 80/443/8080 + dashboard always-on. Xcind ahead.

Same static config; Xcind makes websecure entrypoint + file-provider TLS-conditional and adds dashboard api block only when enabled, drops accessLog. Scind enables dashboard/insecure + accessLog + file provider unconditionally. Xcind ahead.
Bash shell-integration setup script; no Xcind counterpart (part of the Scind-only shell-integration topic; candidate P7 divergence).
Fish shell-integration setup script; no Xcind counterpart (Scind-only shell-integration topic).
Zsh shell-integration setup script; no Xcind counterpart (Scind-only shell-integration topic).
Same living-documentation index/template; Xcind adds a 'Living Documentation Advantage' section and runs via 'make test' (shell suites) vs Scind's cucumber-js.

Same apex-URL topic, conflicting primary-selection: Scind 'multi-export without primary gets no apex' + explicit primary marker vs Xcind positional 'first proxy export' always gets apex; Xcind adds opt-out (empty XCIND_APP_APEX_URL_TEMPLATE) + workspaceless-mode apex. Mirrors ADR 0013/0017 DIVERGED.

Apex-hostname portion overlaps Xcind proxy/apex-routing, but the designation mechanism has no Xcind counterpart: explicit primary:true, multiple-primaries validation error, primary on assigned-port internal alias, SCIND_*_APEX_* env generation. Xcind uses xcind.apex.* labels only, no primary marker.
Dedicated hostname derivation (export/port/service-name override, workspace prefix, edge cases); no standalone Scind feature.
Traefik label/router generation incl. TLS modes, loadbalancer port, xcind-proxy external network; no Scind feature (Scind only references labels inside apex-routing).
Workspace-scoped service aliases, dev-internal network creation, custom service template, hook -f flag, skipped in workspaceless mode; no Scind feature.
App self-declares workspace via XCIND_WORKSPACE late-bind; no Scind counterpart.
Workspace discovery from parent .xcind.sh (XCIND_IS_WORKSPACE) and sourcing precedence; no Scind counterpart.
Injecting app env files into all services (absolute paths, existing-only); Bash-implementation concern with no Scind topic.
Default compose/env-file candidates and XCIND_ENV_FILES back-compat migration; no Scind topic.
Override-file derivation across YAML/HCL/shell/dotfiles and auto-source .xcind.override.sh; no Scind topic.
Compose project-name hook (workspace vs workspaceless, collision prevention); no Scind topic. Relates to ADR-0001/0019 instance isolation.
${APP_ENV} expansion in file patterns and derived overrides; no Scind topic.
.xcind.sh upward-walk discovery, XCIND_APP_ROOT override, workspace-skip; no Scind topic.
Both thin index pages pointing to cli.md + configuration.md; Scind adds an Appendices section (Xcind eng-ref has none).

Same topic, different command model: Scind unified resource-first 'scind <resource> <action>'; Xcind six standalone binaries. Scind-only surface (flavor cmds, workspace clone/generate/destroy, port assign/gc/scan) -> P5; Xcind-ahead (xcind-prompt, provenance --version, --generate-*, application ports/urls/exports) -> P4.

Same concepts (exports, proxied/assigned, templates, TLS, proxy domain) but opposite mechanism: Scind YAML files (explicitly rejects env-var config) vs Xcind .xcind.sh XCIND_* env vars. Xcind-only vars -> P4; Scind flavor/manifest/%VAR% template material -> P5.
Extended CLI walkthroughs; no Xcind eng-reference counterpart. Xcind two-track user docs/ may carry equivalent tutorial material (not chased - flagged for human).
Catalog of CLI error messages + exit codes; Xcind eng-reference has no error catalog (may live in Xcind user docs).
Full worked config examples; Xcind embeds equivalent examples inline in configuration.md rather than a separate appendix.
Version-string/build-metadata provenance schema (XCIND_BUILD_*); no Scind analogue beyond a bare 'scind --version'.
Dev Container generation via --generate-docker-compose-configuration; no Scind counterpart.
Both index the same core doc set; same purpose/framing. Scind adds an appendices pointer. Aligned.

Expected Go/Bash divergence - NOT drift. Scind=Go (Cobra/Viper/Afero, go.mod, ldflags); Xcind=Bash 3.2+/yq/jq/npm/Nix. Shared design decisions transfer (shell-out to docker compose, Traefik); language mechanics do not.
Expected Go/Bash divergence - NOT drift. Scind=Go package tree (cmd/internal/pkg); Xcind=Bash tree (bin/ + lib/xcind/*.bash). Conceptual roles map across; file organization is language-shaped.
Permanent Go/Bash divergence, NOT a P5 gap. Cobra-specific scaffolding (go mod init, cobra command mapping, //go:embed). Xcind has no equivalent scaffolding doc by design.
Permanent Go/Bash divergence, NOT a P5 gap. Go release tooling (GoReleaser). Xcind releases via npm/Nix/git-cliff.
Permanent Go/Bash divergence, NOT a P5 gap. Go build Makefile. Xcind has its own Bash-oriented Makefile in-repo (not in eng-docs).
Go source (package main, cmd/scind/main.go entrypoint). Permanent Go/Bash divergence, NOT a P5 gap - Xcind implements same via bin/ Bash scripts.
Go source (Cobra root command). Permanent Go/Bash divergence, NOT a P5 gap.
Go source (aliases command). Permanent Go/Bash divergence, NOT a P5 gap.
Go source (compose-prefix command, backs the scind-compose shell function). Permanent Go/Bash divergence, NOT a P5 gap.
Go source (shell-integration init). Permanent Go/Bash divergence, NOT a P5 gap.
Go source (validate command). Permanent Go/Bash divergence, NOT a P5 gap.
Go source (utility/helper CLI scaffold). Permanent Go/Bash divergence, NOT a P5 gap.
Go source (workspace resource command). Permanent Go/Bash divergence, NOT a P5 gap - Xcind covers via bin/xcind-workspace.
Go source (app resource command). Permanent Go/Bash divergence, NOT a P5 gap - Xcind covers via bin/xcind-application.
Go source (flavor resource command). Permanent Go/Bash divergence, NOT a P5 gap - flavors are a Scind-only concept (see specs).
Go source (port resource command). Permanent Go/Bash divergence, NOT a P5 gap - Xcind covers via proxy/assigned hooks.
Go source (proxy resource command). Permanent Go/Bash divergence, NOT a P5 gap - Xcind covers via bin/xcind-proxy.
Go source (config command). Permanent Go/Bash divergence, NOT a P5 gap - Xcind covers via xcind-lib.bash config loading.
Go source (typed config structs). Permanent Go/Bash divergence, NOT a P5 gap - Xcind uses env vars, no typed config (see global-context Example A).
Go source (context detection). Permanent Go/Bash divergence, NOT a P5 gap - Xcind covers via context-detection in xcind-lib.bash.
Go source (override generator). Permanent Go/Bash divergence, NOT a P5 gap - Xcind covers via hook-based generation pipeline.
Bash-specific implementation work-record (resolved, ties to ADR-0017). Process artifact, not a canon gap - P7/no-action.
Bash-specific implementation work-record (open; cache-hit hook-replay in xcind-lib.bash). Process artifact - P7/no-action.
Bash-specific implementation work-record (open; cache-ordering staleness in xcind-lib.bash). Process artifact - P7/no-action.
Same purpose (LDS maintenance procedures); differ only in project name. P6 owns process-doc reconciliation.
Scind audit is migration-flavored ('when docs migrated from legacy'); Xcind audit is completeness/accuracy verification. P6 reconciles.
Identical LDS Refine process intro. P6 owns.
Identical LDS Sync process intro; Xcind's sync.md is the process P1 ran. P6 owns (cross-repo sync analog derives from it).
Identical LDS Update process intro. P6 owns.
Xcind Bash release procedure; no Scind counterpart. Process doc - P6.
Xcind source-review coordination plan (work record). P6.

Xcind source-review work record (2026-05-05). P6.
Xcind source-review work record (2026-05-05). P6.
